node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 IDENTIFIER ? IN PYTHON @TECH_IN_SECONDS _SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What are Identifier in Python? We can define identifiers in Python in few ways: An identifier is a user-defined name to represent a variable, a function, a class, a module, or any other object. It is a programmable entity in Python- one with a name. It is a name given to the fundamental building blocks in a progr @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Rules for naming Identifiers in Python So we know what a Python dentifier is. But can we name it anything? Or do certain rules apply? Well, we do have five rules to follow when naming identifiers in Python: swipee>> @TECH_IN_SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 a)A Python identifier can be a combination of lowercase/ uppercase letters, digits, or an underscore. The following characters are valid: Lowercase letters a to Uppercase letters (AtoŽ) Digits (0 to Underscore Some valid names are: myVar var_3 var this_works_too @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 b) An identifier cannot begin with a digit. Some valid names: _9lives lives9 An invalid name: 9lives @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 c) We W”cn use special symbols in the identifier name. Some of these are: $ @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '971 07/ 10 d) We cannot use a keyword as an identifier. Keywords are reserved names in Python and using one of those as a name for an identifier will result in a SyntaxError. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 e) An identifier can be as long as you want. According to the docs, you can have an identifier of infinite length. However, the PEP-8 standard sets a rule that you should limit all lines to a maximum of 79 characters. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be an image of one or more people and text that says '09/ 10 YOUR OPINION MAT MATTERS! COMMENT DOWN YOUR FEEDBACK REGARDING THIS POST @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on November 28, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN_ SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 MACHINE LEARNING FOR PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 MACHINE LEARNING FOR PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What is Machine learning for Python? Machine learning is nothing but using data to make a machine to make intelligent decisions. It is based on recognizing and learning through patterns in data. Intelligent algorithms are then built by extracting, processing, defining, cleaning, arranging and then understanding the data. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Το perform such tedious tasks, why should one use Python? The answer is simple -It - is easy to understand! Adding Python to the implementation process has helped engineers to validate various ideas. and that' s why today DataFlair has come up with a new topic- The importance of Python for Machine Learning @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Machine Learning with Python Python offers all the skillsets that are required for a machine learning or AI project -stability, flexibility and a large number of tools. Python helps developers to be productive and confident about the product that they are manufacturing, from the stages of development to deployment and till the maintenance stage. @TECH_ @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Why Python for Machine Learning? Simple and consistent Libraries and Frameworks Platform Independence Great Community Base @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 1.Simple and consistent Python's simple syntax allows developers to write codes that are reliable, concise and readable. This allows them to only struggle with the solution of the problem and not the code syntax, increasing the productivity of the overall process of development. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 2. Libraries and Frameworks Το help developers in the development process, a number of python libraries and frameworks get involved. It is nothing out a pre-written code that can be used to solve a common progra- mming problem. Python has a rich bank of libraries for machine learning, some of them are- TensorFlow, Keras, and Scikit-learn, Numpy, Scipy, Pandas, Seaborn, etc. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 3. Platform Independence It basically means one can freely shift from one machine to another without making changes to the actual code. This functionality is allowed by python's framework. This is also one of the keys to the popularity of the python language. @TECH_I @TECH_IN_SECONDS wiee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 4. Great Community Base The fact is well that Python's community has grown across the globe, and especially in the world of machine learning and data science. There are active communities that contribute to the large exchange of information which involves solutions to problems. @TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 09, 2021. May be an anime-style image of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! ANY DOUBTS IF YES? LET ME KNOW IN COMMENTS @TECH_IN SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 PACKAGES IN PYTHON Iam TECH_ SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 PACKAGES IN PYTHON Iam TECH_ SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What are Python Packages? In our computer systems, we store our files in organized hierarchies. We don't store them all in one location. Likewise, when our programs grow, we divide it into packages. Like a directory may contain subdirectories and files, a package may contain sub- packages and modules. We have been using modules a lot in the previous lessons. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Remember math, os, and collections? Those were all modules that ship with Python officially. But for now, let's dive into the world of Python packages. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Structure of Python Packages A Python package must have an file in the directory. You may leave it empty, or you may store initialization code in it. But if your directory does not have an _init .py file, it isn't a package; it is just a directory with a bunch of Python scripts. Leaving ini__.py empty is indeed good practice. @TECHIN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Image has modules open, change, and close apart from init .py. Finally, Level has modules start, load, and over, apart from ini__.py Package Module Structure Package Game ini_.py Sub-Package Sound Sub-Package Image Sub-Package init Level init load.py init open.py play.py start.py change.py pause.py load.py close.py over.py @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 How to Import Modules from Packages Pack in Python? A Python package may contain several modules. Το import one of these into your program, you must use the dot operator(.) Note that we don't type the extension, because that isn't what we refer to the module as. The subpackage Level has a module named load too, but there is no clash here. This is because we refer to the module by its fully qualified name. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 How to Create Your Own Python Package? Now, on to the most interesting part. Like we said, Python packages are nothing but a dictionary with sub-packages and modules, and an file. 3 Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 def check(): .n(( Enter a number if a%2==0: 4 5 )) 6 7 print( "Even") else: print( "Odd" 8 check() 9 OUTPUT 10 Enter a number 11 Even 12 @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be an image of one or more people and text.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 08, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says 'A TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN_ SECONDS @TECH SAVE FOR LATER …'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 SEQUENCES IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 SEQUENCES IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What is Python Sequence? A sequence is a group of items with a deterministic ordering. The order in which we put them in is the order in which we get an item out from them. Python offers six types of sequences. Let's discuss them. @TECH IN SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 1. Python Strings A string is a group of characters. Since Python has no provision for arrays, we simply use strings. This is how we declare a string: We can use a pair of single or double quotes. And like we've always said, Python is dynamically-typed. Every string object is of the type 'str'. 2 name= 1# Author: "TECH_IN_SECONDS" "tech_in_seconds" 3 print( type( name OUTPUT: 5 <class 'str'> @TECH_IN @TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 2. Python Lists Since Python does not have arrays, it has lists. A list is an ordered group of items. Το declare it, we use square brackets. A Python list can hold all kinds of items; this is what makes it heterogenous. A list may also contain functions. 1 AUTHOR TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 list ['apple', 'banana' 'mango ] pp 3 print(list[1]) OUTPUT: 'banana' @TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 3. Python Tuples A tuple, in effect, is an immutable group of items. When we say immutable, we mean we cannot change a single value once we declare it. 1 AUTHOR TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 list ('tech', ,'in', 'seconds' 3 pi((list)) print( list)) 4 OUTPUT <cla tuple @TECH_ SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 4. Bytes Sequences The function bytes() returns an immutable bytes object. We dealt with this when we talked Built-in Functions in Python. Let's take a example. AUTHOR 1 TECH_IN_SECONDS 2a=bytes(5) 3 print(a) 4 OUTPUT b'x00x00x00×00×00 @TECH SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 5. Bytes Arrays In that post, we discussed this one too. A bytesarray object is like a bytes object, but it is mutable. It returns an array of the given byte size. 1 1# AUTHOR TECH_IN_SECONDS 2a= bytearray( 4) 3 print(a) 4 OUTPUT 5 bythearray(b'x00lx00x00x00a) @TEC_SEC SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 6. range() objects A range() object lends us a range to iterate on; it gives us a list of numbers. 1 AUTHOR: TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 for i in range(5, print(i) 4 OUTPUT: 3 66 77 8 88 9 9 @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 We took an entire post on Range in Python. Stay tunned for next post. @TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 07, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN_ @TECH_IN_SECON ECONDS NDS @TECH SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 CONSTRUCTOR IN PYTHON Im TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says '01/ 10 CONSTRUCTOR IN PYTHON Im TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What is a Constructor in Python? Python Constructor in object-oriented programming with Python is a special kind of method/function we use to initialize instance members of that class. We can also use it to ensure we have enough resources. Whenever we create an object of that class. the constructor definition is the first to execute. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 How to Declare a Python Constructor 1- Object Creation new is a static class method that lets us control object creation. Whenever we make a call to the class constructor, it makes a call to new While this is a default function for every class, we can definitely play around with it. Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 3 2 class demo: def __new__(self): return dataflair demo() 6 tp(d) OUTPUT <class str'> @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>> pee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 2-Object Initialization (and self -parameter) A constructor is essentially a class function with its name surrounded by double underscores We always call it init 4 5 Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 class citrus: 3 def __init__(self): self.detoxifying def show(self): show( f self.detoxifying == True): print("I detoxify") else: True 6 7 8 do not Detoxify") print(" 10 kumquat= citrus() 11 kumquat 12 OUTPUT I detoxify @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>> swi'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Now statement by statement, let' s see what's happening. init is the constructor here. It takes the self-keyword to tell the interpreter to work with attributes of this object. It should be the first parameter. It takes no parameters. When setting detoxifying to true, we use the self-keyword. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 We have another method show(), we pass self to this too. Then, we create an object as kumquat=citrus(). Finally, we make a call to kumquat.show(). Together, both a constructor. and init form @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 3. New Attributes We can also create a new attribute exclusively for this object and read it when defining values. There is not much you can do once you have already defined the object. 1# Author: Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 kumquat color "Orange" 3 print("I ,kumquat.color) am OUTPUT am Orange @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 Not Declaring a Python Constructor So what happens when we do not explicitly provide a constructor to a class? Can Python handle it? Why don't we try that out? 1# Author 3 4 TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 class color: def shw(self): show( print( "you can see me") me cl =color() l.show() OUTPUT= you can see me 6 7 @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 Here, we did not define a constructor, but Python instantiated that object anyway! This must mean it provides a default constructor that shows up when we do not provide any. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 06, 2021. May be an anime-style image of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! ANY DOUBTS IF YES? LET ME KNOW IN COMMENTS @TECH_IN SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 RECURSION IN PYTHON Iam TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 RECURSION IN PYTHON Iam TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What is Recursion in Python? According to the Oxford English Dictionary, recursion is the repeated application ofa of recursive procedure or definition. Do you see the recursion in this definition itself? They used the word 'recursive' to define 'recursion'. We sense an Easter egg here. Anyway, so as we talk about recursion, we'll take the coolest example first. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Take a look at the logo for PyPy, an imple- mentation of Python with a Just-In-Time Compiler. The snake biting its own tail, feeding itself, is an example of recursion we'd like to give to you. To take a more general example, when our anxiety creates more anxiety for us, it is recursion. In programming, recursion is when a function calls itself. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 How Python Recursion Function Works? Το be clearer, we'll explain how recursion function works in Python. Our factorial() function takes n as argument. The base case that it defines to get out of the Python recursion is when n is equal to 1. In that case, the function will return 1. Otherwise, it will return n multiplied by factorial(n-1). This is a recursive call to itself. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 So this is how it goes: Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 facrial(5) 3=5* factorial(4) 4=5*4* factorial(3) 5=5*4*3* factorial(2) =5*4*3*2* factorial(1) =5*4*3*2*1 8=5*4*3*2 9=5*4*6 10 =524 120 12 13 Hence, we get 120 as the output for factorial(5) @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ RecursionError in Python So far, the code seems to work fine. But now, let's try passing -2 as an argument to this. TECH in <modu e> Author SECONDS 2 Tracebook file "<pyshell#262>" line 1, 3 factorial(-2) 4 file "<pyshell#259>", line 4, in factorial 5 return *farial(n-1) file "<pyshell#259>", line 4, 7 return n*factorial(n-1) file "<pyshell#259>", line 4, in factorial 9 return *factorial(n-1) 10 [The previous line repreated 989 more times] 11 File "<pyshell#259>", line 2, in factorial 12ifn=1: 13 RecursionError in factorial maximum recursion depth exceeded in comparison @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 Python Recursion Function- Pros & Cons Python Recursion Function Advantages With Python recursion, there are some benefits we observe: A recursive code has a cleaner-looking code. Recursion makes it easier to code, as it breaks a task into smaller ones. It is easier to generate a sequence using recursion than by using nested iteration. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 Python Recursion Function Pros & Cons Python Recursion Function Dis-Advantages The flip side of the coin is easy to quote: Although it makes code look cleaner, it may sometimes be hard to follow.. They may be simpler, but recursive calls are expensive. They take up a lot of memory and time. Finally, it isn't as easy to debug a recursive function. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be an image of one or more people and text that says '09/ 10 Keep doing some recursion Python Practice and exercises. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on June 02, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says 'TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER …'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 EXCEPTION IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 EXCEPTION IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 Even if a statement or expression is syntactically correct, it may cause an error when an attempt is made to execute it. Errors detected during execution are called exceptions @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Sr.No. 1 Exception Name & Description Exception Base class for all exceptions 2 Stoplteration Raised when the next() method of an iterator does not point to any object. 3 SystemExit Raised by the sys.exit() function StandardError Base class for all built-in exceptions except Stoplteration and SystemExit. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Sr.No. 5 Exception Name & Description ArithmeticError Base class for all errors that occur for numeric calculation. 6 OverflowError Raised when a calculation exceeds maximum limit for a numeric type. 7 FloatingPointError Raised when a floating point calculation fails. 8 LookupErrorr Base class for all lookup errors.. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Sr.No. Exception Name 9 Description ZeroDivisionErro Raised when division or modulo by zero takes place for all numeric types. 10 AssertionError Raised in case of failure of the Assert statement. 11 AttributeError Raised in case of failure of attribute reference or assignment. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 Sr.No. Exception Name & Description ZeroDivisionError Raised when division or modulo by zero takes place for all numeric types. 10 AssertionError Raised in case of failure of the Assert statement. 11 AttributeError Raised in case of failure of attribute reference or assignment. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 Sr.No. Exception Name & Description 12 RuntimeError Raised when a generated error does not fall into any category. 13 IndentationError Raised when indentation is not specified properly 14 NotlmplementedError Raised when an abstract method that needs to be implemented in an inherited class is not actually implemented. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 It's impossible to include all exceptions in one post,so i covered all the important exceptions with description. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 31, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS NEXT POST @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

#i_know_python #programmingmemes #programming #programmerhumor #programmerlife #codinglife #codingmemes  #coding #programminglife  #javaprogramming #javascriptdeveloper #javascript #pythonprogramming #htmlcoding #webdeveloper #websitedesigner #development #developer

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an anime-style image of text that says '01/ 10 DECORATOR IN PYTHON I TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 What is Python Decorator? Python Decorator function function is a function that adds functionality to another, but does not modify it. In other words Python Decorator wraps another function. This is like a gift wrapper in real life. Also, this is called metaprogramming, because a part of the program tries to modify another at compile time. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Python Decorator with Parameters Το see how decorators fare with parameters, we'll take the example of a function that divides two values. All that our function does is to return the division of two numbers. But when we decorate it, we add functio- nality to deal with the situation where the denomination is 0. Watch how. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swi swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Like you can see, the Python decorator function takes one argument for the funct- ion to decorate. The wrapper here takes the same arguments as does the function to decorate. 1 Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 def devide(a,b): return a/b 3 4 6 5 def decorator( func): def wrapper 7 f 8 b==0: 9 print( "Can 10 return devide by zero' ) 11 return func(a,b) return wrapper @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>> swi'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Finally, we return the function to be decorated instead of calling it. This is because we want to return a value here from the divide() to the wrapper() to the decorator(). @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '1. Python Closure When we call func, it remembers the value of func from the argument to the function decorator()- This is called closure in Python. 2. 2. *args and **kwargs If you don't want to type in the whole list of arguments for the two statements, *args and **kwargs will do the trick for you. *args is a tuple of arguments, and **kwargs is a dictionary of keyword arguments.'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 Pie Syntax in Python If you feel the assignment and calling statements are unnecessary, we've got the pie syntax for you. It's simple; name the decorating function after the symbol, and put this before the function to decorate. Here's an example. SWIPE>>> @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 3 def 4 TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 @decor sayhello(): print( "Hello") 5 sayhello() @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 If you have any douts regarding this post, Let .nnco. me know in comments. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 30, 2021. May be an anime-style image of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! ANY DOUBTS IF YES? LET ME KNOW IN COMMENTS @TECH_IN_ IN_SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 MATHEMATICS IN PYTHON'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 MATHEMATICS IN PYTHON'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 Python is a versatile language that has various applications in the field of data science, web development and scientific computing. We will see how Python has impacted scientific computing with its robust mathematical libraries. So, let's start and explore top Python math libraries. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 What is Maths for Python? Python has become highly popular due to its abundance of libraries. Each Python library is application-oriented that was developed to address problems. Mathematical operations are most preferably carried out in Python due to its focus on utility and minimal programming jargon. There are several libraries that can be used to carry out mathematical operations with Python. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 What is Python Math Library? Following are several Python math libraries 1- Math This is the most basic math module that is available in Python. It covers basic mathem- atical operations like sum, exponential, modulus, etc. This library is not useful when dealing with complex mathematical operations like multiplication of matrices. The calculations performed with the functions of the python math library are also much slower. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 2- Numpy The numpy library in Python is most widely used for carrying out mathematical operations that involve matrices. The most important feature of numpy that sets it apart from other libraries is its ability to perform lightning speed calculations. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 3- SciPy This python math library provides all the scientific tools for Python. It contains various models for mathematical optimization, linear algebra, Fourier Transforms, etc. The numpy module provides the basic data structure of array to the SciPy library. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 4- Statsmodel With the help of this package, you can carry out statistical computations that involve descriptive statistics, inference as well as estimation for the various statistical models. It facilitates efficient statistical exploration of data. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 5- Scikit-learn Machine Learning is an important Mathem- atical aspect of Data Science. Using the various machine learning tools, you can easily classify data and predict the outcomes. For this purpose, Scikit-learn offers various functions to facilitate easy classification, regression, and clustering techniques. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 In this python math post, we had a look at some of the important Python math library. We went through the basic python math library, NumPy, SciPy, statsmodels as well as scikit -learn. There are many more libraries for Mathematical operations in Python and many more are under development. swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 29, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says 'TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. … SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 GENERATOR IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 GENERATOR IN PYTHON TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 Introduction A Python generator is a kind of an iterable, like a Python list or a python tuple. It generates for us a sequence of values that we can iterate on. You can use it to iterate on a for-loop in python, but you can't index it. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Syntax Το create a python generator, we use the yield statement, inside a function, instead of the return statement. 1# Author TECH_IN_SECONDS 2 def counter(): 3 4 5 6 while( <=10): yieldi i += 1 @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Working of Python Generator Το understand how this code works, we'll start with the for-loop. For each item in the Python generator (each item that it yields), it prints it, here. We begin with i=1. So, the first item that it yields is 1. The for-loop prints this because of our print statement. Then, we increment Ito 2. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 And the process follows until i is incremented to 11. Then, the while loop's condition becomes False. However, if you forget the statement to increment it results in an infinite generator. This is because a Python generator needs to hold only one value at a time. So, there are no memory restrictions. @TECH_IN_SECONDS @TECH_IN swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 Python Generator vS Function Now, to compare a generator to a function, we first talk about return and Python yield. When the interpreter reaches the return statement in a function, it stops executing the Python Generator function and executes the statement after the function call. >>>> @TECH_IN_SECONDS @T”CH_IN_ wipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 However, when it reaches the Python yield statement in a generator, it yields the value to the iterable. Finally, it gets back to the generator to continue for the next value. Also, when a function stops executing, its local variables are destroyed. This is not the same with a Python generator. @TECH_IN_SECONDS @TECH_IN swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 Overall; Now that you know the benefits of python generator over a list or over a function, you will understand its importance. Some things, we can do with a generator, with a function, or even with a list comprehension. But using a Python generator is the most efficient. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 SHARE YOUR THOUGHTS REGARDING THIS POST @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 28, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN @TECH ECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 COUNTER IN PYTHON O TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 COUNTER IN PYTHON O TECH_IN_SECONDS SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '02/ 10 Counter in Python Python Counter, like the other three cont- ainers we mentioned above is a subclass of dict'. It keeps a count of the number of occurrences of any value in the container. Simply speaking, if you add the value 'hello' thrice in the container, it will remember that you added it thrice. So, Counter counts hashable objects in Python. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 Why use Python counter 1- It allows you to count the items in an iterable list. 2- Arithmetic operations like addition, subtraction, intersection, and union can be easily performed on a Counter. 3- A Counter can also count elements from another counter @TECH_IN_SECONDS wipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 Understanding Counter Python Counter takes in input a ist. tuple, dictionary, string, which are all iterable objects, and it will give you output that will have the count of each element. Syntax: Counter(list) @TECH_IN_SECONDS swi wiee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 Counter with String In Python, everything is an object and string is an object too. Python string can be created simply by enclosing characters in the double quote. Python does not support a character type. These are treated as strings of length one, also considered as a substring. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 Counter with List A list is an iterable object that has its elements inside square brackets. The elements in the list when given to the Counter will be converted to a hashtable objects wh @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 Counter with Dictionary A dictionary has elements as key/value pair, and they are written inside curly brackets. Once the dictionary is given to the Counter, it will be converted to a hashtable objects wherein the elements will become keys, and the values will be the count of the elements from the dictionary given. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 Counter with Tuple Tuple is a collection of objects separated by commas inside round brackets. Counter will give you the count of each of the elements in the tuple given. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 Methods Available on Python Counter elements() most_common(value) common(value) subtract(): update(): @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 26, 2021. May be an anime-style image of text that says '10/ 10 TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! ANY DOUBTS IF YES? LET ME KNOW IN COMMENTS @TECH_IN_SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER'.
Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 INHERITANCE IN PYTHON Im TECH_IN_SECONDS'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be a cartoon of text that says '01/ 10 INHERITANCE IN PYTHON Im TECH_IN_SECONDS'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of one or more people and text that says '02/ 10 Inheritance in Python Inheritance is the capability of one class to derive or inherit the properties from another class. The benefits of inheritance are: @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '03/ 10 1- It represents real-world relationships well. 2- It provides reusability of a code. We don't have to write the same code again and again. 3- Also, it allows us to add more features to a class without modifying it. 4- It is transitive in nature, which means that if class B inherits from another class A, then all the subclasses of B would automatically inherit from class A. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '04/ 10 In this type of programming mainly two types of classes are used. Parent /Sur/Base class Child/Sub/Derived class Parent/Super/Base class-The class which is inherited by another class is called Parent or Super or base class. Child/Sub/Derived class- class which inherits the property of another class is called child or Sub or Derived class. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '05/ 10 What is object class? Like Java Object class, in Python (from version 3.x), object is root of all classes. In Python 3.x, "class Test(object)" and "class Test" are same. In Python 2.x, "class Test(object)" creates a class with object as parent (called new style class) and "class Test" creates old style class (without object parent). @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '06/ 10 Different forms of Inheritance: 1. Single inheritance: When a child class inherits from only one parent class, it is called single inheritance. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '07/ 10 2. Multiple inheritance: When a child class inherits from multiple parent classes, it is called multiplei nheritance. Unlike Java and like C++, Python supports multiple inheritance.W specify all parent classes as a comma-separated list in the bracket. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '08/ 10 3. Multilevel inheritance: When we have a child and grandchild relationship. 4. Hybrid inheritance: This form combines more than one form of inheritance. Basically, it is a blend of more than one type of inheritance. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be an image of text that says '09/ 10 5. 5. Hierachical inheritance: This form combines more than one form of inheritance. Basically, it is a blend of more than one type of inheritance. @TECH_IN_SECONDS swipee>>'.

Photo by Tech_In_Seconds on May 25, 2021. May be a cartoon of one or more people and text that says 'TURN ON POST NOTIFICATIONS! LEARN PYTHON BASIC TO ADVANCED? Follow for more Learnable posts. @TECH_IN SECONDS SAVE FOR LATER …'.
